description:
GOAL: Worker pool + task scheduler — fz processes as tasks on a small
native pool. v1 ships pool size 1; abstractions assume task != thread.
Cooperative scheduling between tasks.

## Architectural framing (user, 2026-05-11)

LIBDISPATCH-STYLE: many fz processes (tasks) serviced by a small native
worker pool. NOT "one OS thread per process". The Process struct from
fz-ul4.11.32 represents a task; this ticket adds the workers + queue +
scheduler that drives Processes through quanta.

Pool size = configurable knob via `Runtime::new(workers: usize)`,
default 1. Going to pool size N>1 later is a Scheduler-internals change,
not architectural. v1 needs to ship the abstractions right (TLS per
worker, atomic state on Process) even with one worker.

## Decisions captured

- ONE shared address space across processes (NOT BEAM-style separate
  spaces). Pointers valid across process boundaries; the constraint is
  GC ownership.
- Per-process heap = bounded GC pauses.
- CPS codegen makes a process trivially-representable: continuation
  ptr + per-process heap. No fibers, no stackful coroutines.
- Cooperative scheduling: tasks yield at receive (.19.3) and at GC
  safepoints (.11.31). Optional `yield()` builtin is a follow-up.
- Pool size 1 for v1; configurable.
- Atom table: global shared with cheap mutex (.19.6); not per-process.
- Schemas: per-CompiledModule, Arc<[Schema]>-style sharing across
  workers / processes (planned in .11.32 follow-up).

## Prerequisites

- fz-ul4.11.32 — Process struct with heap + builders; CURRENT_PROCESS
  TLS replaces 5 ir_codegen.rs TLS slots. SINGLE-WORKER USE
  ESTABLISHED. This ticket adds the multi-task scheduler ON TOP.

## Deliverables

### Runtime + Worker (new src/runtime/runtime.rs or src/runtime/mod.rs)

```rust
pub struct Runtime {
    workers: Vec<Worker>,                 // size = workers arg
    run_queue: Arc<Mutex<VecDeque<PidId>>>, // ready tasks
    tasks: Arc<Mutex<HashMap<PidId, Box<Process>>>>,
    next_pid: AtomicU32,
}

impl Runtime {
    pub fn new(workers: usize) -> Self;   // default 1
    pub fn spawn(&self, fn_id: FnId) -> PidId;
    pub fn run_until_idle(&self);         // blocks until all tasks done
}

struct Worker {
    handle: thread::JoinHandle<()>,
}

fn worker_main(rq: Arc<Mutex<...>>, tasks: Arc<Mutex<...>>) {
    loop {
        let pid = pull_ready(&rq);
        let process = take_task(&tasks, pid);
        CURRENT_PROCESS.with(|c| c.set(&mut *process as *mut Process));
        run_one_quantum(&mut process);   // runs until yield / halt / receive
        CURRENT_PROCESS.with(|c| c.set(null_mut()));
        match process.state {
            Ready => { put_task_back(&tasks, pid, process); enqueue(&rq, pid); }
            Blocked => { put_task_back(&tasks, pid, process); /* no enqueue */ }
            Exited => { /* drop */ }
        }
    }
}
```

For pool size 1, this becomes a single worker thread. For pool size N,
multiple workers race the run_queue; each pulls one task at a time.
Scheduler invariant: a task is in the run_queue OR being run by exactly
one worker OR Blocked. Never simultaneously on two workers.

### Process additions (extends .11.32's Process)

```rust
pub struct Process {
    // from .11.32:
    pub heap: Heap,
    pub halt_value: i64,
    pub map_builder, bs_builder, vec_builder, closure_builder, ...

    // new here:
    pub pid: PidId,
    pub state: State,                    // Ready | Running | Blocked | Exited
    pub next_frame: *mut u8,             // current continuation; null = exited
    pub mailbox: VecDeque<FzValue>,      // populated by .19.3
}

pub enum State { Ready, Running, Blocked, Exited }
```

state is plain enum for v1 (one worker, no contention); becomes AtomicEnum
when pool size > 1 lands. Document.

### Quantum mechanics

```rust
fn run_one_quantum(p: &mut Process) {
    while !p.next_frame.is_null() {
        // .11.31 will add GC safepoint check here
        let schema_id = read_schema_id(p.next_frame);
        let fn_ptr = COMPILED.fn_ptrs[schema_id];
        let next = unsafe { fn_ptr(p.next_frame, ...) };
        p.next_frame = next;
        // Future: cooperative-yield builtin can set p.state = Ready and break.
    }
    p.state = Exited;
}
```

For v1: a quantum runs until halt or receive-block. No preemption.
Single-task tight loops monopolize the worker — documented; preemption
(reductions counter) is a follow-up.

### Bridge for existing tests

`CompiledModule::run(fn_id)` and `run_in(fn_id, &mut Process)` from
.11.32 keep working. They are the single-task path. The Runtime
abstraction is in addition, not replacement. Existing tests stay green.

## Worker pool size 1 vs N tradeoffs

- N=1: tasks interleave cooperatively on one OS thread. No locks
  contended; mutex around run_queue is uncontended. Trivial debug.
- N=2+: workers race run_queue; mutex contention starts to matter,
  but trivial Mutex<VecDeque> is fine until measurement shows otherwise.
- Lock-free run queue (crossbeam) is a perf follow-up.

## Yield mechanism

- HALT (process exits): trampoline returns null; state -> Exited.
- RECEIVE BLOCK (.19.3): a `fz_receive(ctx)` builtin sets state ->
  Blocked, returns sentinel that breaks the trampoline. Worker yields.
- GC SAFEPOINT (.11.31): in-quantum; doesn't yield to other tasks
  itself, but documents pause time per task is bounded.
- COOPERATIVE YIELD (optional): a `yield()` builtin that sets state ->
  Ready, breaks trampoline. Decide at impl time.

## Tests

- Pool size 1: spawn 3 tasks, each running a counter loop with explicit
  yields; verify all make progress. (Tests cooperative interleaving on
  one worker.)
- Pool size 2 (if not too painful): same test; verify completion is
  ~half the wall time. (Tests multi-worker actually works.)
- A task that halts is removed from the runtime; live task count drops.
- Single-task degenerate case via existing CompiledModule.run() path
  still works (all SAMPLE-based tests).
- Two tasks with independent map allocations on pool size 1 — confirms
  .11.32's per-Process builders work under cooperative interleaving.

## Risks

- Cooperative-only means a tight loop without yield monopolizes its
  worker. v1 acceptable; preemption (reductions counter) is a follow-up.
- Pool size N: mailbox/state need atomic protection. v1 ships N=1 so
  this is documented but not implemented; will revisit when N>1 use
  arrives.
- Mutex on run_queue: not lock-free; revisit if measurements show
  contention under N>1.

## Out of scope

- spawn / self / pid surface (.19.2 — uses this infra).
- Mailbox + send / receive (.19.3).
- Per-process GC verification (.19.4).
- Preemption via reductions counter (follow-up).
- Lock-free run queue (perf follow-up).
- Multi-worker correctness under contention (not implemented for v1;
  ship pool size 1; revisit when scaling).
title: Process / scheduler infrastructure (process = continuation ptr)
